description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_enumeration

Deliverable: migrate .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md to the SpecLedEx schema:
- spec-scenarios block: reshape each scenario to given:/when:/then: arrays (bespoke shape fails parse with 'expected array').
- spec-verification block: replace execute:/source_file: stubs with command: and target: entries; every requirement gets a verification entry.
- spec-meta: flip status: draft -> active once the file validates.
- keep prose, intent, id, surface, and decisions links intact.

Verification:
- mix spec.validate emits ZERO errors for mutagen.mutation_enumeration (baseline: 6 errors on this file).
- subject status is active in .spec/state.json.

Out of Scope (files): every .spec.md EXCEPT mutation_enumeration.spec.md; lib/**; test/**.
Allowed touches: .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md only.
Out of Scope (intent): no realized_by bindings (phase2); no code/test edits; do not alter requirement ids or delete requirements.
Merge gate: mix spec.validate (0 errors for mutagen.mutation_enumeration) && mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 0eb050fa-440a-426e-9f51-bfd4d1ee50de
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/0eb050fa-440a-426e-9f51-bfd4d1ee50de.jsonl
Commit: dc3f319

Summary:
Migrated .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md to the SpecLedEx schema.
- spec-scenarios (s1-s9): reshaped given:/when:/then: from scalar block
  literals to string arrays. Quoted entries that begin with backticks or
  contain ": " to keep YAML valid. Prose content unchanged.
- spec-verification (v1-v5): replaced `command:` key with `target:`; kept
  kind/execute/covers. Added v6 (covers r6) and v7 (covers r8) so every
  requirement now has a verification entry — both target the existing
  mutation_enumerator_test.exs which already exercises the no-disk-read (r6)
  and end_line/end_column (r8) behavior.
- spec-meta: status draft -> active.
- id, surface, decisions links, intent, and all requirement text/ids left
  intact. No requirements added, removed, or renumbered. No realized_by
  bindings added (phase2).

Files touched:
- .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md (allowed touch)
- .spec/state.json (tracked generated index; regenerated by mix spec.validate,
  NOT hand-edited)

Tests added: none — this is a spec-file migration. The "tests" are validation.

Stubs flipped: n/a (no execute:false -> true; verification entries already
execute: true).

Merge gate results:
- mix spec.validate -> ZERO errors AND zero warnings for subject
  mutagen.mutation_enumeration (baseline was 6 errors). Other unmigrated
  subjects (mutators, scope_resolution, etc.) still show errors as expected
  this phase.
- subject status is "active" in .spec/state.json (verified via jq).
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors -> clean.
- mix test -> 640 tests, 1 failure. The single failure is
  coverage_runner_test.exs:318 (subject mutagen.coverage, OUT OF SCOPE for
  this ticket), an order-dependent tmp-leak: a sibling test
  (beam_cache_test) leaves a `mutagen_ex_beam_cache_test_*` tmp entry that
  the coverage r7 no-disk-writes assertion then trips on. Running
  coverage_runner_test.exs in isolation after clearing tmp -> 16 tests, 0
  failures. A markdown-only spec edit + regenerated state.json cannot affect
  runtime tmp behavior; this is a pre-existing cross-test isolation flake,
  not a regression from this ticket. Flagging for the verifier rather than
  touching test/** (forbidden by Out of Scope).

Out of Scope honored: only .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md and the
generated .spec/state.json changed (git diff --stat confirms 2 files). No
lib/**, no test/**, no other .spec.md files touched.

title: migrate mutagen.mutation_enumeration to SpecLedEx schema (6 errors)
